front: What is the definition of UX Metrics?
back: UX Metrics are quantifiable measures used to evaluate the quality of a user's interaction with a product, system, or service. They provide objective data to assess how well a design meets user needs and business objectives.
NPS measures customer loyalty by asking how likely users are to recommend the product on a scale of 0-10. Score = %Promoters (9-10) - %Detractors (0-6). Range: -100 to +100.
CSAT measures immediate satisfaction with a product, feature, or interaction typically using a 1-5 or 1-10 scale. Formula: (Satisfied customers / Total responses) × 100.
CES measures how easy it is for users to accomplish a task, usually on a 1-7 scale. Lower scores indicate easier interactions. Formula: Sum of all scores / Number of responses.
SUS is a 10-question questionnaire giving a score from 0-100. Scores above 68 are considered above average. It's a standard usability measure developed by John Brooke.
Behavioral metrics measure actual user actions including clicks, page views, time on task, task completion rate, error rate, and conversion rates. They track what users do.
Attitudinal metrics measure user perceptions, opinions, and attitudes through surveys, interviews, and questionnaires. They capture what users think and feel.
Task Success Rate measures the percentage of users who successfully complete a given task. Formula: (Successful completions / Total attempts) × 100.
Time on Task measures how long it takes users to complete a specific task. It's measured in seconds or minutes using usability testing or analytics tools.
Error Rate measures the frequency of user errors during task completion. Formula: (Number of errors / Total opportunities for error) × 100.
Conversion Rate measures the percentage of users who complete a desired action (purchase, sign-up, download). Formula: (Conversions / Total visitors) × 100.
Bounce Rate measures the percentage of visitors who leave after viewing only one page without taking any action. Lower bounce rates indicate better engagement.
HEART is a framework for measuring user experience: Happiness (satisfaction), Engagement (involvement), Adoption (new users), Retention (returning users), and Task Success (effectiveness).

front: What is the Pirate Metrics Framework (AARRR)?
back: AARRR stands for Acquisition (users arrive), Activation (sign up), Retention (return), Referral (invite others), and Revenue (monetize). It's also called Pirate Metrics.
A/B Testing is a method of comparing two versions of a webpage or app to determine which performs better. Users are randomly split between versions and statistical significance is measured.
